faq.question: How long can breastmilk sit out?
faq.answer: Freshly expressed breastmilk can sit at room temperature (77°F/25°C or below) for up to 4 hours. In a hot room, use within 1-2 hours. Once baby has started feeding from a bottle, use within 2 hours.

faq.question: Can I mix fresh and frozen breastmilk?
faq.answer: Yes, but cool the fresh milk in the fridge first before adding to frozen. Never add warm milk directly to frozen. Label with the date of the oldest milk. The storage clock starts from the oldest milk's date.

faq.question: How do I thaw frozen breastmilk?
faq.answer: Best: overnight in the fridge. Quick: run under warm (not hot) water or use a bottle warmer. Never microwave — it creates hot spots and destroys nutrients. Once thawed, use within 24 hours. Never refreeze.
